63	/// Options contains all the possible behaviors and flags for mv.
64	///
65	/// All options are public so that the options can be programmatically
66	/// constructed by other crates, such as nushell. That means that this struct is
67	/// part of our public API. It should therefore not be changed without good reason.
68	///
69	/// The fields are documented with the arguments that determine their value.
70	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Eq, PartialEq)]
71	pub struct Options {
72	    /// specifies overwrite behavior
73	    /// '-n' '--no-clobber'
74	    /// '-i' '--interactive'
75	    /// '-f' '--force'
76	    pub overwrite: OverwriteMode,
77	
78	    /// `--backup[=CONTROL]`, `-b`
79	    pub backup: BackupMode,
80	
81	    /// '-S' --suffix' backup suffix
82	    pub suffix: String,
83	
84	    /// Available update mode "--update-mode=all|none|older"
85	    pub update: UpdateMode,
86	
87	    /// Specifies target directory
88	    /// '-t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY'
89	    pub target_dir: Option<OsString>,
90	
91	    /// Treat destination as a normal file
92	    /// '-T, --no-target-directory
93	    pub no_target_dir: bool,
94	
95	    /// '-v, --verbose'
96	    pub verbose: bool,
97	
98	    /// '--strip-trailing-slashes'
99	    pub strip_slashes: bool,
100	
101	    /// '-g, --progress'
102	    pub progress_bar: bool,
103	
104	    /// `--debug`
105	    pub debug: bool,
106	
107	    /// `-Z, --context`
108	    pub context: Option<String>,
109	}

111	impl Default for Options {
112	    fn default() -> Self {
113	        Self {
114	            overwrite: OverwriteMode::default(),
115	            backup: BackupMode::default(),
116	            suffix: backup_control::DEFAULT_BACKUP_SUFFIX.to_owned(),
117	            update: UpdateMode::default(),
118	            target_dir: None,
119	            no_target_dir: false,
120	            verbose: false,
121	            strip_slashes: false,
122	            progress_bar: false,
123	            debug: false,
124	            context: None,
125	        }
126	    }

129	/// specifies behavior of the overwrite flag
130	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Default)]
131	pub enum OverwriteMode {
132	    /// '-n' '--no-clobber'   do not overwrite
133	    NoClobber,
134	    /// '-i' '--interactive'  prompt before overwrite
135	    Interactive,
136	    ///'-f' '--force'         overwrite without prompt
137	    #[default]
138	    Force,
139	}

142	static OPT_INTERACTIVE: &str = "interactive";
143	static OPT_NO_CLOBBER: &str = "no-clobber";
144	static OPT_STRIP_TRAILING_SLASHES: &str = "strip-trailing-slashes";

154	#[uucore::main]
155	pub fn uumain(args: impl uucore::Args) -> UResult<()> {
156	    let matches = uu_app().get_matches_from_localized(args);
157	
158	    let files: Vec<OsString> = matches
159	        .get_many::<OsString>(ARG_FILES)
160	        .unwrap_or_default()
161	        .cloned()
162	        .collect();
163	
164	    if files.len() == 1 && !matches.contains_id(OPT_TARGET_DIRECTORY) {
165	        let err = uu_app().error(
166	            ErrorKind::TooFewValues,
167	            translate!("mv-error-insufficient-arguments", "arg_files" => ARG_FILES),
168	        );
169	        uucore::clap_localization::handle_clap_error_with_exit_code(err, uucore::util_name(), 1);
170	    }
171	
172	    let overwrite_mode = determine_overwrite_mode(&matches);
173	    let backup_mode = backup_control::determine_backup_mode(&matches)?;
174	    let update_mode = update_control::determine_update_mode(&matches);
175	
176	    if backup_mode != BackupMode::None
177	        && (overwrite_mode == OverwriteMode::NoClobber
178	            || update_mode == UpdateMode::None
179	            || update_mode == UpdateMode::NoneFail)
180	    {
181	        return Err(UUsageError::new(
182	            1,
183	            translate!("mv-error-backup-with-no-clobber"),
184	        ));
185	    }
186	
187	    let backup_suffix = backup_control::determine_backup_suffix(&matches);
188	
189	    let target_dir = matches
190	        .get_one::<OsString>(OPT_TARGET_DIRECTORY)
191	        .map(OsString::from);
192	
193	    if let Some(ref maybe_dir) = target_dir {
194	        if !Path::new(&maybe_dir).is_dir() {
195	            return Err(MvError::TargetNotADirectory(maybe_dir.quote().to_string()).into());
196	        }
197	    }
198	
199	    // Handle -Z and --context options
200	    // If -Z is used, use the default context (empty string)
201	    // If --context=value is used, use that specific value
202	    let context = if matches.get_flag(OPT_SELINUX) {
203	        Some(String::new())
204	    } else {
205	        matches.get_one::<String>(OPT_CONTEXT).cloned()
206	    };
207	
208	    let opts = Options {
209	        overwrite: overwrite_mode,
210	        backup: backup_mode,
211	        suffix: backup_suffix,
212	        update: update_mode,
213	        target_dir,
214	        no_target_dir: matches.get_flag(OPT_NO_TARGET_DIRECTORY),
215	        verbose: matches.get_flag(OPT_VERBOSE) || matches.get_flag(OPT_DEBUG),
216	        strip_slashes: matches.get_flag(OPT_STRIP_TRAILING_SLASHES),
217	        progress_bar: matches.get_flag(OPT_PROGRESS),
218	        debug: matches.get_flag(OPT_DEBUG),
219	        context,
220	    };
221	
222	    mv(&files[..], &opts)
223	}

334	
335	fn determine_overwrite_mode(matches: &ArgMatches) -> OverwriteMode {
336	    // This does not exactly match the GNU implementation:
337	    // The GNU mv defaults to Force, but if more than one of the
338	    // overwrite options are supplied, only the last takes effect.
339	    // To default to no-clobber in that situation seems safer:
340	    //
341	    if matches.get_flag(OPT_NO_CLOBBER) {
342	        OverwriteMode::NoClobber
343	    } else if matches.get_flag(OPT_INTERACTIVE) {
344	        OverwriteMode::Interactive
345	    } else {
346	        OverwriteMode::Force
347	    }
348	}
349	
350	fn parse_paths(files: &[OsString], opts: &Options) -> Vec<PathBuf> {
351	    let paths = files.iter().map(Path::new);
352	
353	    if opts.strip_slashes {
354	        paths
355	            .map(|p| p.components().as_path().to_owned())
356	            .collect::<Vec<PathBuf>>()
357	    } else {
358	        paths.map(|p| p.to_owned()).collect::<Vec<PathBuf>>()
359	    }
360	}
361	
362	fn handle_two_paths(source: &Path, target: &Path, opts: &Options) -> UResult<()> {
363	    if opts.backup == BackupMode::Simple && source_is_target_backup(source, target, &opts.suffix) {
364	        return Err(io::Error::new(
365	            io::ErrorKind::NotFound,
366	            translate!("mv-error-backup-might-destroy-source", "target" => target.quote(), "source" => source.quote()),
367	        )
368	        .into());
369	    }
370	    if source.symlink_metadata().is_err() {
371	        return Err(if path_ends_with_terminator(source) {
372	            MvError::CannotStatNotADirectory(source.quote().to_string()).into()
373	        } else {
374	            MvError::NoSuchFile(source.quote().to_string()).into()
375	        });
376	    }
377	
378	    let target_is_dir = target.is_dir();
379	    let source_is_dir = source.is_dir();
380	
381	    if path_ends_with_terminator(target)
382	        && (!target_is_dir && !source_is_dir)
383	        && !opts.no_target_dir
384	        && opts.update != UpdateMode::IfOlder
385	    {
386	        return Err(MvError::FailedToAccessNotADirectory(target.quote().to_string()).into());
387	    }
388	
389	    assert_not_same_file(source, target, target_is_dir, opts)?;
390	
391	    if target_is_dir {
392	        if opts.no_target_dir {
393	            if source.is_dir() {
394	                #[cfg(unix)]
395	                let (mut hardlink_tracker, hardlink_scanner) = create_hardlink_context();
396	                #[cfg(unix)]
397	                let hardlink_params = (Some(&mut hardlink_tracker), Some(&hardlink_scanner));
398	                #[cfg(not(unix))]
399	                let hardlink_params = (None, None);
400	
401	                rename(
402	                    source,
403	                    target,
404	                    opts,
405	                    None,
406	                    hardlink_params.0,
407	                    hardlink_params.1,
408	                )
409	                .map_err_context(|| {
410	                    translate!("mv-error-cannot-move", "source" => source.quote(), "target" => target.quote())
411	                })
412	            } else {
413	                Err(MvError::DirectoryToNonDirectory(target.quote().to_string()).into())
414	            }
415	        } else {
416	            move_files_into_dir(&[source.to_path_buf()], target, opts)
417	        }
418	    } else if target.exists() && source.is_dir() {
419	        match opts.overwrite {
420	            OverwriteMode::NoClobber => return Ok(()),
421	            OverwriteMode::Interactive => {
422	                if !prompt_yes!(
423	                    "{}",
424	                    translate!("mv-prompt-overwrite", "target" => target.quote())
425	                ) {
426	                    return Err(io::Error::other("").into());
427	                }
428	            }
429	            OverwriteMode::Force => {}
430	        }
431	        Err(MvError::NonDirectoryToDirectory(
432	            source.quote().to_string(),
433	            target.quote().to_string(),
434	        )
435	        .into())
436	    } else {
437	        #[cfg(unix)]
438	        let (mut hardlink_tracker, hardlink_scanner) = create_hardlink_context();
439	        #[cfg(unix)]
440	        let hardlink_params = (Some(&mut hardlink_tracker), Some(&hardlink_scanner));
441	        #[cfg(not(unix))]
442	        let hardlink_params = (None, None);
443	
444	        rename(
445	            source,
446	            target,
447	            opts,
448	            None,
449	            hardlink_params.0,
450	            hardlink_params.1,
451	        )
452	        .map_err(|e| USimpleError::new(1, format!("{e}")))
453	    }
454	}
455	
456	fn assert_not_same_file(
457	    source: &Path,
458	    target: &Path,
459	    target_is_dir: bool,
460	    opts: &Options,
461	) -> UResult<()> {
462	    // we'll compare canonicalized_source and canonicalized_target for same file detection
463	    let canonicalized_source = match canonicalize(
464	        absolute(source)?,
465	        MissingHandling::Normal,
466	        ResolveMode::Logical,
467	    ) {
468	        Ok(source) if source.exists() => source,
469	        _ => absolute(source)?, // file or symlink target doesn't exist but its absolute path is still used for comparison
470	    };
471	
472	    // special case if the target exists, is a directory, and the `-T` flag wasn't used
473	    let target_is_dir = target_is_dir && !opts.no_target_dir;
474	    let canonicalized_target = if target_is_dir {
475	        // `mv source_file target_dir` => target_dir/source_file
476	        // canonicalize the path that exists (target directory) and join the source file name
477	        canonicalize(
478	            absolute(target)?,
479	            MissingHandling::Normal,
480	            ResolveMode::Logical,
481	        )?
482	        .join(source.file_name().unwrap_or_default())
483	    } else {
484	        // `mv source target_dir/target` => target_dir/target
485	        // we canonicalize target_dir and join /target
486	        match absolute(target)?.parent() {
487	            Some(parent) if parent.to_str() != Some("") => {
488	                canonicalize(parent, MissingHandling::Normal, ResolveMode::Logical)?
489	                    .join(target.file_name().unwrap_or_default())
490	            }
491	            // path.parent() returns Some("") or None if there's no parent
492	            _ => absolute(target)?, // absolute paths should always have a parent, but we'll fall back just in case
493	        }
494	    };
495	
496	    let same_file = (canonicalized_source.eq(&canonicalized_target)
497	        || are_hardlinks_to_same_file(source, target)
498	        || are_hardlinks_or_one_way_symlink_to_same_file(source, target))
499	        && opts.backup == BackupMode::None;
500	
501	    // get the expected target path to show in errors
502	    // this is based on the argument and not canonicalized
503	    let target_display = match source.file_name() {
504	        Some(file_name) if target_is_dir => {
505	            // join target_dir/source_file in a platform-independent manner
506	            let mut path = target
507	                .display()
508	                .to_string()
509	                .trim_end_matches('/')
510	                .to_owned();
511	
512	            path.push('/');
513	            path.push_str(&file_name.to_string_lossy());
514	
515	            path.quote().to_string()
516	        }
517	        _ => target.quote().to_string(),
518	    };
519	
520	    if same_file
521	        && (canonicalized_source.eq(&canonicalized_target)
522	            || source.eq(Path::new("."))
523	            || source.ends_with("/.")
524	            || source.is_file())
525	    {
526	        return Err(MvError::SameFile(source.quote().to_string(), target_display).into());
527	    } else if (same_file || canonicalized_target.starts_with(canonicalized_source))
528	        // don't error if we're moving a symlink of a directory into itself
529	        && !source.is_symlink()
530	    {
531	        return Err(
532	            MvError::SelfTargetSubdirectory(source.quote().to_string(), target_display).into(),
533	        );
534	    }
535	    Ok(())
536	}
537	
538	fn handle_multiple_paths(paths: &[PathBuf], opts: &Options) -> UResult<()> {
539	    if opts.no_target_dir {
540	        return Err(UUsageError::new(
541	            1,
542	            translate!("mv-error-extra-operand", "operand" => paths.last().unwrap().quote()),
543	        ));
544	    }
545	    let target_dir = paths.last().unwrap();
546	    let sources = &paths[..paths.len() - 1];
547	
548	    move_files_into_dir(sources, target_dir, opts)
549	}
550	
551	/// Execute the mv command. This moves 'source' to 'target', where
552	/// 'target' is a directory. If 'target' does not exist, and source is a single
553	/// file or directory, then 'source' will be renamed to 'target'.
554	pub fn mv(files: &[OsString], opts: &Options) -> UResult<()> {
555	    let paths = parse_paths(files, opts);
556	
557	    if let Some(ref name) = opts.target_dir {
558	        return move_files_into_dir(&paths, &PathBuf::from(name), opts);
559	    }
560	
561	    match paths.len() {
562	        2 => handle_two_paths(&paths[0], &paths[1], opts),
563	        _ => handle_multiple_paths(&paths, opts),
564	    }
565	}
566	
567	#[allow(clippy::cognitive_complexity)]
568	fn move_files_into_dir(files: &[PathBuf], target_dir: &Path, options: &Options) -> UResult<()> {
569	    // remember the moved destinations for further usage
570	    let mut moved_destinations: HashSet<PathBuf> = HashSet::with_capacity(files.len());
571	    // Create hardlink tracking context
572	    #[cfg(unix)]
573	    let (mut hardlink_tracker, hardlink_scanner) = {
574	        let (tracker, mut scanner) = create_hardlink_context();
575	
576	        // Use hardlink options
577	        let hardlink_options = HardlinkOptions {
578	            verbose: options.verbose || options.debug,
579	        };
580	
581	        // Pre-scan files if needed
582	        if let Err(e) = scanner.scan_files(files, &hardlink_options) {
583	            if hardlink_options.verbose {
584	                eprintln!("mv: warning: failed to scan files for hardlinks: {e}");
585	                eprintln!("mv: continuing without hardlink preservation");
586	            } else {
587	                // Show warning in non-verbose mode for serious errors
588	                eprintln!(
589	                    "mv: warning: hardlink scanning failed, continuing without hardlink preservation"
590	                );
591	            }
592	            // Continue without hardlink tracking on scan failure
593	            // This provides graceful degradation rather than failing completely
594	        }
595	
596	        (tracker, scanner)
597	    };
598	
599	    if !target_dir.is_dir() {
600	        return Err(MvError::NotADirectory(target_dir.quote().to_string()).into());
601	    }
602	
603	    let multi_progress = options.progress_bar.then(MultiProgress::new);
604	
605	    let count_progress = if let Some(ref multi_progress) = multi_progress {
606	        if files.len() > 1 {
607	            Some(
608	                multi_progress.add(
609	                    ProgressBar::new(files.len().try_into().unwrap()).with_style(
610	                        ProgressStyle::with_template(&format!(
611	                            "{} {{msg}} {{wide_bar}} {{pos}}/{{len}}",
612	                            translate!("mv-progress-moving")
613	                        ))
614	                        .unwrap(),
615	                    ),
616	                ),
617	            )
618	        } else {
619	            None
620	        }
621	    } else {
622	        None
623	    };
624	
625	    for sourcepath in files {
626	        if sourcepath.symlink_metadata().is_err() {
627	            show!(MvError::NoSuchFile(sourcepath.quote().to_string()));
628	            continue;
629	        }
630	
631	        if let Some(ref pb) = count_progress {
632	            let msg = format!("{} (scanning hardlinks)", sourcepath.to_string_lossy());
633	            pb.set_message(msg);
634	        }
635	
636	        let targetpath = match sourcepath.file_name() {
637	            Some(name) => target_dir.join(name),
638	            None => {
639	                show!(MvError::NoSuchFile(sourcepath.quote().to_string()));
640	                continue;
641	            }
642	        };
643	
644	        if moved_destinations.contains(&targetpath) && options.backup != BackupMode::Numbered {
645	            // If the target file was already created in this mv call, do not overwrite
646	            show!(USimpleError::new(
647	                1,
648	                translate!("mv-error-will-not-overwrite-just-created", "target" => targetpath.display(), "source" => sourcepath.display()),
649	            ));
650	            continue;
651	        }
652	
653	        // Check if we have mv dir1 dir2 dir2
654	        // And generate an error if this is the case
655	        if let Err(e) = assert_not_same_file(sourcepath, target_dir, true, options) {
656	            show!(e);
657	            continue;
658	        }
659	
660	        #[cfg(unix)]
661	        let hardlink_params = (Some(&mut hardlink_tracker), Some(&hardlink_scanner));
662	        #[cfg(not(unix))]
663	        let hardlink_params = (None, None);
664	
665	        match rename(
666	            sourcepath,
667	            &targetpath,
668	            options,
669	            multi_progress.as_ref(),
670	            hardlink_params.0,
671	            hardlink_params.1,
672	        ) {
673	            Err(e) if e.to_string().is_empty() => set_exit_code(1),
674	            Err(e) => {
675	                let e = e.map_err_context(|| {
676	                    translate!("mv-error-cannot-move", "source" => sourcepath.quote(), "target" => targetpath.quote())
677	                });
678	                match multi_progress {
679	                    Some(ref pb) => pb.suspend(|| show!(e)),
680	                    None => show!(e),
681	                }
682	            }
683	            Ok(()) => (),
684	        }
685	        if let Some(ref pb) = count_progress {
686	            pb.inc(1);
687	        }
688	        moved_destinations.insert(targetpath.clone());
689	    }
690	    Ok(())
691	}

693	fn rename(
694	    from: &Path,
695	    to: &Path,
696	    opts: &Options,
697	    multi_progress: Option<&MultiProgress>,
698	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_tracker: Option<&mut HardlinkTracker>,
699	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_scanner: Option<&HardlinkGroupScanner>,
700	    #[cfg(not(unix))] _hardlink_tracker: Option<()>,
701	    #[cfg(not(unix))] _hardlink_scanner: Option<()>,
702	) -> io::Result<()> {
703	    let mut backup_path = None;
704	
705	    if to.exists() {
706	        if opts.update == UpdateMode::None {
707	            if opts.debug {
708	                println!("{}", translate!("mv-debug-skipped", "target" => to.quote()));
709	            }
710	            return Ok(());
711	        }
712	
713	        if (opts.update == UpdateMode::IfOlder)
714	            && fs::metadata(from)?.modified()? <= fs::metadata(to)?.modified()?
715	        {
716	            return Ok(());
717	        }
718	
719	        if opts.update == UpdateMode::NoneFail {
720	            let err_msg = translate!("mv-error-not-replacing", "target" => to.quote());
721	            return Err(io::Error::other(err_msg));
722	        }
723	
724	        match opts.overwrite {
725	            OverwriteMode::NoClobber => {
726	                if opts.debug {
727	                    println!("{}", translate!("mv-debug-skipped", "target" => to.quote()));
728	                }
729	                return Ok(());
730	            }
731	            OverwriteMode::Interactive => {
732	                if !prompt_yes!(
733	                    "{}",
734	                    translate!("mv-prompt-overwrite", "target" => to.quote())
735	                ) {
736	                    return Err(io::Error::other(""));
737	                }
738	            }
739	            OverwriteMode::Force => {}
740	        }
741	
742	        backup_path = backup_control::get_backup_path(opts.backup, to, &opts.suffix);
743	        if let Some(ref backup_path) = backup_path {
744	            // For backup renames, we don't need to track hardlinks as we're just moving the existing file
745	            rename_with_fallback(to, backup_path, multi_progress, None, None)?;
746	        }
747	    }
748	
749	    // "to" may no longer exist if it was backed up
750	    if to.exists() && to.is_dir() {
751	        // normalize behavior between *nix and windows
752	        if from.is_dir() {
753	            if is_empty_dir(to) {
754	                fs::remove_dir(to)?;
755	            } else {
756	                return Err(io::Error::other(translate!("mv-error-directory-not-empty")));
757	            }
758	        }
759	    }
760	
761	    #[cfg(unix)]
762	    {
763	        rename_with_fallback(from, to, multi_progress, hardlink_tracker, hardlink_scanner)?;
764	    }
765	    #[cfg(not(unix))]
766	    {
767	        rename_with_fallback(from, to, multi_progress, None, None)?;
768	    }
769	
770	    #[cfg(feature = "selinux")]
771	    if let Some(ref context) = opts.context {
772	        set_selinux_security_context(to, Some(context))
773	            .map_err(|e| io::Error::other(e.to_string()))?;
774	    }
775	
776	    if opts.verbose {
777	        let message = match backup_path {
778	            Some(path) => {
779	                translate!("mv-verbose-renamed-with-backup", "from" => from.quote(), "to" => to.quote(), "backup" => path.quote())
780	            }
781	            None => translate!("mv-verbose-renamed", "from" => from.quote(), "to" => to.quote()),
782	        };
783	
784	        match multi_progress {
785	            Some(pb) => pb.suspend(|| {
786	                println!("{message}");
787	            }),
788	            None => println!("{message}"),
789	        }
790	    }
791	    Ok(())
792	}

804	/// A wrapper around `fs::rename`, so that if it fails, we try falling back on
805	/// copying and removing.
806	fn rename_with_fallback(
807	    from: &Path,
808	    to: &Path,
809	    multi_progress: Option<&MultiProgress>,
810	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_tracker: Option<&mut HardlinkTracker>,
811	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_scanner: Option<&HardlinkGroupScanner>,
812	    #[cfg(not(unix))] _hardlink_tracker: Option<()>,
813	    #[cfg(not(unix))] _hardlink_scanner: Option<()>,
814	) -> io::Result<()> {
815	    fs::rename(from, to).or_else(|err| {
816	        #[cfg(windows)]
817	        const EXDEV: i32 = windows_sys::Win32::Foundation::ERROR_NOT_SAME_DEVICE as _;
818	        #[cfg(unix)]
819	        const EXDEV: i32 = libc::EXDEV as _;
820	
821	        // We will only copy if:
822	        // 1. Files are on different devices (EXDEV error)
823	        // 2. On Windows, if the target file exists and source file is opened by another process
824	        //    (MoveFileExW fails with "Access Denied" even if the source file has FILE_SHARE_DELETE permission)
825	        let should_fallback =
826	            matches!(err.raw_os_error(), Some(EXDEV)) || (from.is_file() && can_delete_file(from));
827	        if !should_fallback {
828	            return Err(err);
829	        }
830	        // Get metadata without following symlinks
831	        let metadata = from.symlink_metadata()?;
832	        let file_type = metadata.file_type();
833	        if file_type.is_symlink() {
834	            rename_symlink_fallback(from, to)
835	        } else if file_type.is_dir() {
836	            #[cfg(unix)]
837	            {
838	                with_optional_hardlink_context(
839	                    hardlink_tracker,
840	                    hardlink_scanner,
841	                    |tracker, scanner| {
842	                        rename_dir_fallback(from, to, multi_progress, Some(tracker), Some(scanner))
843	                    },
844	                )
845	            }
846	            #[cfg(not(unix))]
847	            {
848	                rename_dir_fallback(from, to, multi_progress)
849	            }
850	        } else if is_fifo(file_type) {
851	            rename_fifo_fallback(from, to)
852	        } else {
853	            #[cfg(unix)]
854	            {
855	                with_optional_hardlink_context(
856	                    hardlink_tracker,
857	                    hardlink_scanner,
858	                    |tracker, scanner| rename_file_fallback(from, to, Some(tracker), Some(scanner)),
859	                )
860	            }
861	            #[cfg(not(unix))]
862	            {
863	                rename_file_fallback(from, to)
864	            }
865	        }
866	    })
867	}

883	/// Move the given symlink to the given destination. On Windows, dangling
884	/// symlinks return an error.
885	#[cfg(unix)]
886	fn rename_symlink_fallback(from: &Path, to: &Path) -> io::Result<()> {

888	    unix::fs::symlink(path_symlink_points_to, to).and_then(|_| fs::remove_file(from))

891	#[cfg(windows)]
892	fn rename_symlink_fallback(from: &Path, to: &Path) -> io::Result<()> {
893	    let path_symlink_points_to = fs::read_link(from)?;
894	    if path_symlink_points_to.exists() {
895	        if path_symlink_points_to.is_dir() {
896	            windows::fs::symlink_dir(&path_symlink_points_to, to)?;
897	        } else {
898	            windows::fs::symlink_file(&path_symlink_points_to, to)?;
899	        }
900	        fs::remove_file(from)
901	    } else {
902	        Err(io::Error::new(
903	            io::ErrorKind::NotFound,
904	            translate!("mv-error-dangling-symlink"),
905	        ))
906	    }
907	}

918	fn rename_dir_fallback(
919	    from: &Path,
920	    to: &Path,
921	    multi_progress: Option<&MultiProgress>,
922	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_tracker: Option<&mut HardlinkTracker>,
923	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_scanner: Option<&HardlinkGroupScanner>,
924	) -> io::Result<()> {
925	    // We remove the destination directory if it exists to match the
926	    // behavior of `fs::rename`. As far as I can tell, `fs_extra`'s
927	    // `move_dir` would otherwise behave differently.
928	    if to.exists() {
929	        fs::remove_dir_all(to)?;
930	    }
931	
932	    // Calculate total size of directory
933	    // Silently degrades:
934	    //    If finding the total size fails for whatever reason,
935	    //    the progress bar wont be shown for this file / dir.
936	    //    (Move will probably fail due to permission error later?)
937	    let total_size = dir_get_size(from).ok();
938	
939	    let progress_bar = match (multi_progress, total_size) {
940	        (Some(multi_progress), Some(total_size)) => {
941	            let template = "{msg}: [{elapsed_precise}] {wide_bar} {bytes:>7}/{total_bytes:7}";
942	            let style = ProgressStyle::with_template(template).unwrap();
943	            let bar = ProgressBar::new(total_size).with_style(style);
944	            Some(multi_progress.add(bar))
945	        }
946	        (_, _) => None,
947	    };
948	
949	    #[cfg(all(unix, not(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "redox"))))]
950	    let xattrs = fsxattr::retrieve_xattrs(from).unwrap_or_else(|_| HashMap::new());
951	
952	    // Use directory copying (with or without hardlink support)
953	    let result = copy_dir_contents(
954	        from,
955	        to,
956	        #[cfg(unix)]
957	        hardlink_tracker,
958	        #[cfg(unix)]
959	        hardlink_scanner,
960	        progress_bar.as_ref(),
961	    );
962	
963	    #[cfg(all(unix, not(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "redox"))))]
964	    fsxattr::apply_xattrs(to, xattrs)?;
965	
966	    result?;
967	
968	    // Remove the source directory after successful copy
969	    fs::remove_dir_all(from)?;
970	
971	    Ok(())
972	}

974	/// Copy directory recursively, optionally preserving hardlinks
975	fn copy_dir_contents(
976	    from: &Path,
977	    to: &Path,
978	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_tracker: Option<&mut HardlinkTracker>,
979	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_scanner: Option<&HardlinkGroupScanner>,
980	    progress_bar: Option<&ProgressBar>,
981	) -> io::Result<()> {
982	    // Create the destination directory
983	    fs::create_dir_all(to)?;
984	
985	    // Recursively copy contents
986	    #[cfg(unix)]
987	    {
988	        if let (Some(tracker), Some(scanner)) = (hardlink_tracker, hardlink_scanner) {
989	            copy_dir_contents_recursive(from, to, tracker, scanner, progress_bar)?;
990	        }
991	    }
992	    #[cfg(not(unix))]
993	    {
994	        copy_dir_contents_recursive(from, to, progress_bar)?;
995	    }
996	
997	    Ok(())
998	}

1000	fn copy_dir_contents_recursive(
1001	    from_dir: &Path,
1002	    to_dir: &Path,
1003	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_tracker: &mut HardlinkTracker,
1004	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_scanner: &HardlinkGroupScanner,
1005	    progress_bar: Option<&ProgressBar>,
1006	) -> io::Result<()> {
1007	    let entries = fs::read_dir(from_dir)?;
1008	
1009	    for entry in entries {
1010	        let entry = entry?;
1011	        let from_path = entry.path();
1012	        let file_name = from_path.file_name().unwrap();
1013	        let to_path = to_dir.join(file_name);
1014	
1015	        if let Some(pb) = progress_bar {
1016	            pb.set_message(from_path.to_string_lossy().to_string());
1017	        }
1018	
1019	        if from_path.is_dir() {
1020	            // Recursively copy subdirectory
1021	            fs::create_dir_all(&to_path)?;
1022	            copy_dir_contents_recursive(
1023	                &from_path,
1024	                &to_path,
1025	                #[cfg(unix)]
1026	                hardlink_tracker,
1027	                #[cfg(unix)]
1028	                hardlink_scanner,
1029	                progress_bar,
1030	            )?;
1031	        } else {
1032	            // Copy file with or without hardlink support based on platform
1033	            #[cfg(unix)]
1034	            {
1035	                copy_file_with_hardlinks_helper(
1036	                    &from_path,
1037	                    &to_path,
1038	                    hardlink_tracker,
1039	                    hardlink_scanner,
1040	                )?;
1041	            }
1042	            #[cfg(not(unix))]
1043	            {
1044	                fs::copy(&from_path, &to_path)?;
1045	            }
1046	        }
1047	
1048	        if let Some(pb) = progress_bar {
1049	            if let Ok(metadata) = from_path.metadata() {
1050	                pb.inc(metadata.len());
1051	            }
1052	        }
1053	    }
1054	
1055	    Ok(())
1056	}

1058	#[cfg(unix)]
1059	fn copy_file_with_hardlinks_helper(
1060	    from: &Path,
1061	    to: &Path,
1062	    hardlink_tracker: &mut HardlinkTracker,
1063	    hardlink_scanner: &HardlinkGroupScanner,
1064	) -> io::Result<()> {
1065	    // Check if this file should be a hardlink to an already-copied file
1066	    use crate::hardlink::HardlinkOptions;
1067	    let hardlink_options = HardlinkOptions::default();
1068	    // Create a hardlink instead of copying
1069	    if let Some(existing_target) =
1070	        hardlink_tracker.check_hardlink(from, to, hardlink_scanner, &hardlink_options)?
1071	    {
1072	        fs::hard_link(&existing_target, to)?;
1073	        return Ok(());
1074	    }
1075	
1076	    // Regular file copy
1077	    #[cfg(all(unix, not(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "redox"))))]
1078	    {
1079	        fs::copy(from, to).and_then(|_| fsxattr::copy_xattrs(&from, &to))?;
1080	    }
1081	    #[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "redox"))]
1082	    {
1083	        fs::copy(from, to)?;
1084	    }
1085	
1086	    Ok(())
1087	}

1089	fn rename_file_fallback(
1090	    from: &Path,
1091	    to: &Path,
1092	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_tracker: Option<&mut HardlinkTracker>,
1093	    #[cfg(unix)] hardlink_scanner: Option<&HardlinkGroupScanner>,
1094	) -> io::Result<()> {
1095	    // Remove existing target file if it exists
1096	    if to.is_symlink() {
1097	        fs::remove_file(to).map_err(|err| {
1098	            let inter_device_msg = translate!("mv-error-inter-device-move-failed", "from" => from.display(), "to" => to.display(), "err" => err);
1099	            io::Error::new(err.kind(), inter_device_msg)
1100	        })?;
1101	    } else if to.exists() {
1102	        // For non-symlinks, just remove the file without special error handling
1103	        fs::remove_file(to)?;
1104	    }
1105	
1106	    // Check if this file is part of a hardlink group and if so, create a hardlink instead of copying
1107	    #[cfg(unix)]
1108	    {
1109	        if let (Some(tracker), Some(scanner)) = (hardlink_tracker, hardlink_scanner) {
1110	            use crate::hardlink::HardlinkOptions;
1111	            let hardlink_options = HardlinkOptions::default();
1112	            if let Some(existing_target) =
1113	                tracker.check_hardlink(from, to, scanner, &hardlink_options)?
1114	            {
1115	                // Create a hardlink to the first moved file instead of copying
1116	                fs::hard_link(&existing_target, to)?;
1117	                fs::remove_file(from)?;
1118	                return Ok(());
1119	            }
1120	        }
1121	    }
1122	
1123	    // Regular file copy
1124	    #[cfg(all(unix, not(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "redox"))))]
1125	    fs::copy(from, to)
1126	        .and_then(|_| fsxattr::copy_xattrs(&from, &to))
1127	        .and_then(|_| fs::remove_file(from))
1128	        .map_err(|err| io::Error::new(err.kind(), translate!("mv-error-permission-denied")))?;
1129	    #[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "redox", not(unix)))]
1130	    fs::copy(from, to)
1131	        .and_then(|_| fs::remove_file(from))
1132	        .map_err(|err| io::Error::new(err.kind(), translate!("mv-error-permission-denied")))?;
1133	    Ok(())
1134	}

1140	/// Checks if a file can be deleted by attempting to open it with delete permissions.
1141	#[cfg(windows)]
1142	fn can_delete_file(path: &Path) -> bool {
1143	    use std::{
1144	        os::windows::ffi::OsStrExt as _,
1145	        ptr::{null, null_mut},
1146	    };
1147	
1148	    use windows_sys::Win32::{
1149	        Foundation::{CloseHandle, INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE},
1150	        Storage::FileSystem::{
1151	            CreateFileW, DELETE, FILE_ATTRIBUTE_NORMAL, FILE_SHARE_DELETE, FILE_SHARE_READ,
1152	            FILE_SHARE_WRITE, OPEN_EXISTING,
1153	        },
1154	    };
1155	
1156	    let wide_path = path

The problem is in the `rename` function around lines 693-792. When moving a directory symlink (`source`), the code checks if the target exists and is a directory. The key issue is at line 750:

```rust
if to.exists() && to.is_dir() {
```

When `to` is a symlink to a directory, `to.exists()` returns `true` (it follows symlinks) and `to.is_dir()` returns `true` (follows symlinks). So for the case where `from` is a symlink to a directory (i.e., `from.is_dir()` returns `true` because `from` is a symlink to a directory), the code tries to check if `to` is empty and remove it. But the issue is: when `from` is a symlink to a directory, `fs::rename` from `from` to `to` should work (rename the symlink itself, not the directory), but the code first tries to remove `to` (thinking it's a directory that needs to be replaced).

Wait, let me re-read more carefully. The bug is about moving directory symbolic links. Let me think about the scenario:

- `/run/wrappers/bin-tmp` is a symlink to a directory
- `/run/wrappers/bin` is a symlink to a directory

When GNU mv moves `/run/wrappers/bin-tmp` to `/run/wrappers/bin`, it renames the symlink. uutils mv sees that `to` exists and is a directory (following symlinks) and tries to check if it's empty, then tries to remove it, which fails with "Directory not empty" because the target directory the symlink points to has contents.

The problem is that when dealing with symlinks, we should use `symlink_metadata()` instead of `exists()`/`is_dir()` which follow symlinks.


When both `from` and `to` are symlinks to directories:
- `to.exists()` returns `true` (follows symlink)  
- `to.is_dir()` returns `true` (follows symlink)
- `from.is_dir()` returns `true` (follows symlink)

So the code enters the block and tries to remove `to` (which is a symlink pointing to a directory with contents), leading to "Directory not empty" error.

But the correct behavior (GNU mv behavior) would be: if both `from` and `to` are symlinks, just rename the symlink `from` to become `to` (overwriting `to` which is just a symlink). We shouldn't be treating symlinks as directories.

The fix needs to:
1. Check `to` using `symlink_metadata()` to determine if `to` itself is a symlink
2. If `from` is a symlink, we should just rename the symlink, not try to merge directories

So at line 391: `if target_is_dir` matches, and since `opts.no_target_dir` is false by default, the code goes to `move_files_into_dir`, which moves the source into the target directory.

The fix should check whether the target is a symlink and treat it as a non-directory in that case. The behavior should be:
- If `to` is a symlink, treat it just like a symlink — replace it with `from`
- Only treat `to` as a directory (and move `from` into it) if `to` is actually a real directory, not a symlink to one

The `mv` command on line 168 is:

```bash
mv --no-target-directory "/run/wrappers/bin-tmp" "/run/wrappers/bin"
```

Here's the surrounding context (~20 lines before and after):

**Before (lines ~148-167):** The script symlinks the new wrapper directory to a temporary path using `ln --symbolic --force --no-dereference "$wrapperDir" "/run/wrappers/bin-tmp"`, then sets up a log file at `LOGMVFILE="/home/luana/nixos-config/log-stat-mv"`. It logs `stat` and `ls` output for both `bin-tmp` and `bin`, and writes "mv runs now" to the log. A commented-out line references a Nix store path for an alternative `mv` binary, with the note: "atomically replace the symlink."

**Line 168:** The `mv` command with `--no-target-directory` moves the temporary symlink into place as `/run/wrappers/bin`.

**After (lines ~169-189):** It logs "mv has finished," records post-move `stat` and `ls` for both `bin-tmp` and `bin`, writes "error-post," then removes the old wrapper directory with `rm --force --recursive "$old"`. The `else` branch (for initial setup) simply creates a direct symlink without the atomic replacement dance.

content.text:
Now I understand! The mv command uses `--no-target-directory` (which is `-T`). The script does:

```bash
mv --no-target-directory "/run/wrappers/bin-tmp" "/run/wrappers/bin"
```

With `--no-target-directory`, mv should treat `/run/wrappers/bin` as a destination name, NOT as a directory. Even though `bin` is a symlink to a directory, with `-T` it should replace the symlink `bin` with `bin-tmp`.

So the problem is in `handle_two_paths`:
- `target.is_dir()` returns `true` because `bin` symlink points to a directory
- But with `--no-target-directory`, we should NOT treat it as a directory
- However, line 391-396: even with `no_target_dir`, it requires `source.is_dir()` to be true to proceed with the rename. If `source.is_dir()` is `true`, it enters the `rename` function.

Wait, let me re-check. In `handle_two_paths`:

```rust
let target_is_dir = target.is_dir(); // true for symlink to dir
...
if target_is_dir {                    // enters this branch
    if opts.no_target_dir {          // enters this because -T is set
        if source.is_dir() {         // THIS is the key - source is a symlink to dir, so source.is_dir() returns true
            // tries to rename 
        } else {
            Err(MvError::DirectoryToNonDirectory(...))
        }
    }
}
```

So with `-T`, `source.is_dir()` returns `true` (source is symlink to directory), and it calls `rename()`. Then in `rename()`, the check at line 750 fires:

```rust
if to.exists() && to.is_dir() {    // true, bin symlink exists and points to dir
    if from.is_dir() {             // true, bin-tmp symlink points to dir
        if is_empty_dir(to) {      // bin's target directory is NOT empty
            ...
        } else {
            return Err(io::Error::other("Directory not empty")); // ERROR!
        }
    }
}
```

So the bug is: when both source and target are symlinks (not actual directories), and `-T`/`--no-target-directory` is used, the code incorrectly tries to treat the target as a directory that needs to be removed, instead of just letting `fs::rename` replace the symlink.

The fix should be in the `rename` function at line 750: we should check whether `to` is a symlink using `symlink_metadata()`, and if so, skip the directory merging/removal logic.

Similarly, in `handle_two_paths`, the `target_is_dir` check could also take into account whether the target is a symlink (though the `-T` path already handles the case, the issue is deeper in `rename`).

Let me look at this more carefully. The core issue is that `to.is_dir()` follows symlinks, and when `to` is a symlink to a directory, we should not follow it — we should treat it as a symlink (a non-directory).

Wait, I also need to check `handle_two_paths`. When `target.is_dir()` returns true but target is a symlink, and `no_target_dir` is false, it goes to `move_files_into_dir`. That's actually the correct behavior — if `bin` is a symlink to a directory and you do `mv bin-tmp bin` (without `-T`), it should move `bin-tmp` into the directory (matching GNU behavior). That's fine.

But wait — `handle_two_paths` also has the `assert_not_same_file` check and other logic. Let me check if there are any other issues with symlinks in `handle_two_paths`. Let me re-read lines 378-414:

```rust
let target_is_dir = target.is_dir();
let source_is_dir = source.is_dir();

if path_ends_with_terminator(target)
    && (!target_is_dir && !source_is_dir)
    ...
```

These use `is_dir()` which follows symlinks. This should be fine because the semantics are about what the path resolves to.

The `target_is_dir` check at line 391:
- Without `-T`: `target_is_dir` is true, `no_target_dir` is false → `move_files_into_dir` → correct (moves into the directory)
- With `-T`: `target_is_dir` is true, `no_target_dir` is true → tries to do a direct rename (which works now with the fix)

The `source.is_dir()` check at line 393: with `-T`, this is true because source is a symlink to directory. This is correct — we want to rename the symlink.

Actually, wait. I need to check if my fix regresses this specific case. Let me check what the original code did for this case:

Originally, with real directory `from` and symlink `to`:
- `to.exists() && to.is_dir()` → true (follows symlink)
- `from.is_dir()` → true (real dir)
- Then it tries `is_empty_dir(to)` (checks the directory the symlink points to) 
- If not empty, returns "Directory not empty"
- If empty, removes the directory and then `fs::rename` works (renaming a real dir to a path where nothing exists)

With my fix:
- `to.exists() && to.is_dir() && !to.is_symlink()` → false (because `to` is a symlink)
- Skips the directory cleanup block
- Falls through to `fs::rename(from, to)` which fails because you can't rename a directory over a symlink

So my fix changes behavior here: previously it would either remove the empty target dir and rename, or error with "Directory not empty". Now it tries rename and gets "Not a directory" from the OS.

The GNU behavior (for `mv -T real_dir symlink_to_dir`) is "cannot overwrite non-directory 'linkB' with directory 'realA'". That's the error we should produce.

Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the `handle_two_paths` flow for this case. When `from` is a real directory and `to` is a symlink to directory, with `-T`:
- `target.is_dir()` → true
- `opts.no_target_dir` → true  
- `source.is_dir()` → true

It goes into the `rename` call at line 401. But the logic should really be: if `to` is a symlink, it's not a directory in the context of "can I rename a directory over it?". The target is effectively a non-directory (a symlink), so we should get a proper error.

Let me check what happens at `handle_two_paths` level. The `target_is_dir` variable is set from `target.is_dir()` which follows symlinks. If we change it to `target.is_dir() && !target.is_symlink()` (for the purpose of determining if target should be treated as a directory), then with `-T`:
- `target_is_dir` would be false
- Falls to the else at line 418: `target.exists() && source.is_dir()` → true
- Returns `NonDirectoryToDirectory` error

But `NonDirectoryToDirectory` error message is "cannot overwrite non-directory with directory" which matches GNU!

Hmm, but changing `target_is_dir` to not follow symlinks would also affect the non-`-T` case: when `target` is a symlink to a directory without `-T`, it should move the source INTO the directory. Currently it does this because `target_is_dir` is true and it goes to `move_files_into_dir`. If we change `target_is_dir` to not follow symlinks, it would not move into the directory anymore, which would break the normal `mv file symlink_to_dir` behavior.

So the fix in `rename()` is the right place. The only regression is that `mv -T real_dir symlink_to_dir` now produces "Not a directory" instead of "Directory not empty". Both are error cases, just different error messages. And this edge case wasn't part of the original bug report.

Hmm! The first test now fails with "cannot overwrite non-directory 'bin' with directory 'bin-tmp'"! This is wrong — we should be able to move a symlink to a directory over another symlink.

The problem is that `source.is_dir()` returns true when source is a symlink to a directory. So in `handle_two_paths` with my change:

- `target_is_dir` = `target.is_dir() && !(true && target.is_symlink())` = `true && !(true && true)` = `false`
- Falls to `else if target.exists() && source.is_dir()` → `true && true` = true
- Returns `NonDirectoryToDirectory` error

So I need to also check if `source` is a symlink. The "cannot overwrite non-directory with directory" error should only be emitted when `source` is a REAL directory, not a symlink to one.

Let me fix:

content.text:
Now let me also check what `MvError::NonDirectoryToDirectory` is used for. Looking at line 435:
```rust
Err(MvError::NonDirectoryToDirectory(
    source.quote().to_string(),
    target.quote().to_string(),
)
```

This means "cannot overwrite non-directory X with directory Y". This case is for when `target` exists (but was determined not to be a directory via `target.is_dir()`) and `source` is a directory (`source.is_dir()` returns true).

With the `target_is_dir` check based on `target.is_dir()` which follows symlinks, if target is a symlink to a directory, `target.is_dir()` returns true, so `target_is_dir` is true and this error is never reached. That means for the case of `mv -T real_dir symlink_to_dir`, the original code enters the `rename` function, which has the directory-cleanup logic that returns "Directory not empty".

The only thing my `rename` fix changes for this case is that it now skips the cleanup and lets `fs::rename` fail, producing "Not a directory" OS error instead of "Directory not empty". Both are error cases. Neither produces "cannot overwrite non-directory with directory" which GNU produces for this specific case (real_dir over symlink).

For the actual bug (both are symlinks to directories), my fix correctly makes `fs::rename` succeed because we skip the directory cleanup logic. This is the right fix.
